description: theHarvester: The quintessential tool designed for gathering e-mail accounts, subdomain names, virtual hosts, open ports, and employee names from an organization.

commands.explanation: Executes an immediate baseline scraping query targeting a specific external search engine to yield exposed emails/subdomains.
A highly noisy extraction method instructing the tool to aggressively query every single supported index (LinkedIn, Bing, Google, DuckDuckGo) simultaneously.
Restricts parsing arrays exactly to the indicated numerical depth limit of Search Engine results (e.g. Top 500 pages) to prevent aggressive IP blocking.
Augments passive scraping explicitly executing a highly aggressive active dictionary brute-force attacking the Target's primary DNS tables.
Forces the resolution arrays to continuously ping subdomains verifying explicitly if a DNS CNAME point exists allowing for Subdomain Takeovers.
Automates standard text dumping explicitly writing both highly structured JSON files and HTML tables concurrently directly into the current directory.
Leverages Microsoft Bing reverse-indexing attempting to unmask distinct auxiliary Virtual Hosts sharing the exact same IP stack.
Integrates natively directly into the Shodan infrastructure verifying all located host architectures against known open ports instantly.
Channels the Python processing HTTP streams seamlessly overriding outbound tracking connecting through defined Proxy architectures.
Modifies the inherent socket stack forcing all explicit active DNS interrogations directly out through the specified custom recursive DNS server.
